risks: Cosmos DB serverless is restricted to a single Azure region; no multi-region failover is possible, reducing reliability for a production Q&A API
No VNet integration or private endpoints configured for any resource; Azure OpenAI, Cosmos DB, Key Vault, and APIM are all publicly accessible
Container App references image from docqaregistry.azurecr.io but no Azure Container Registry resource is included in the plan
Key Vault has publicNetworkAccess set to Enabled, which expands the attack surface beyond what managed identity alone mitigates
No availability zone redundancy configured for any resource in the plan
Container Apps environment appLogsConfiguration specifies log-analytics destination but does not include the workspaceResourceId linking to the Log Analytics workspace
APIM Basic SKU does not include built-in VNet integration; upgrading to Standard or Premium would be needed for network-isolated deployments
No explicit RBAC role assignments defined for the managed identity to access Azure OpenAI, Cosmos DB, or Key Vault data planes
correctionsRecommended: Add an Azure Container Registry resource and configure the Container App with registry credentials or managed identity-based AcrPull role assignment
Add private endpoints for Cosmos DB, Azure OpenAI, and Key Vault to restrict data-plane access to the VNet
Include RBAC role assignment resources granting the user-assigned managed identity Cognitive Services OpenAI User, Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor, and Key Vault Secrets User roles
Set workspaceResourceId on the Container Apps environment appLogsConfiguration to connect logs to the Log Analytics workspace
Consider adding a Container Registry resource with managed identity pull to avoid image pull failures at deployment time
Evaluate enabling availability zones on the Container Apps environment and APIM for improved reliability
Add resource tags for cost tracking, environment identification, and operational governance
Set Key Vault publicNetworkAccess to Disabled once private endpoint connectivity is established
hardDependencies: Container App image pull requires a reachable container registry (docqaregistry.azurecr.io) with the docqa-api:latest image available; registry is not provisioned in this plan
